context.goal: Validate functional correctness of the combinational adder for typical operand values.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['a', 'b', 'Cin']
Apply a set of random legal input vectors to a, b, and Cin and compare outputs to a reference model.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Allow sufficient time for inputs to settle before sampling outputs.
No assumptions about internal pipeline stages.

context.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['a', 'b']
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.

context.goal: Stress test the carry propagation logic with patterns that trigger maximum carry chain length.
stimulus_intent: Drive a = 0xFFFF, b = 0x0001, Cin = 0 to generate a full carry chain
Drive a = 0x8000, b = 0x8000, Cin = 1 to test MSB carry generation
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after inputs have stabilized; no fixed latency assumed.

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.
